title: quercus aegilops subsp. ithaburensis
intro: Quercus aegilops subsp. ithaburensis, also known as the Turkish oak or Ithaburensis oak, is a subspecies of the Quercus aegilops tree, native to the eastern Mediterranean region. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus aegilops subsp. ithaburensis include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer and neuroprotective properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed to reap the plant's medicinal benefits.
